items.title: USDA finalizes tougher SNAP retailer stocking rules; enforcement starts in November
items.summary: USDA’s Food and Nutrition Service finalized a rule requiring SNAP-authorized retailers to stock at least 7 distinct staple-food varieties in each of 4 staple categories, up from 3 varieties previously. Retailers must also offer at least 1 perishable variety in 3 of the 4 staple categories (up from 2), with the rule effective July 7, 2026 and required implementation by Nov. 4, 2026.

items.title: USDA says SNAP retailers must carry more ‘real food’ starting fall 2026
items.summary: USDA announced updated SNAP retailer stocking standards requiring 7 varieties across the 4 staple food categories (protein, grains, dairy, fruits/vegetables), aiming to increase whole and perishable foods and close loopholes that counted some snack foods. USDA noted SNAP retailer payments exceed $90 billion per year (about $236 million per day) and said it has taken action against nearly 3,200 retailers for stocking-standard issues.

items.title: HUD outlines FY 2026 Housing Choice Voucher funding and key set-aside deadlines
items.summary: HUD’s FY 2026 Housing Choice Voucher funding notice includes $34.557 billion for HAP renewals, $2.836 billion for administrative fees, and a $400 million HAP set-aside (among other line items). Housing agencies seeking several set-aside categories (including Unforeseen Circumstances, Portability, and Project-Based Vouchers) have a deadline of June 26, 2026; blended administrative fee rate requests are due July 10, 2026.

items.title: CMS seeks public comments on Medicaid HCBS quality measures (due June 8)
items.summary: CMS opened a public comment period on Home and Community-Based Services (HCBS) measures maintenance and development that runs from May 8, 2026 through June 8, 2026. Stakeholders can submit feedback during this window, which CMS posts as a current Medicaid-related public comment opportunity.

items.title: Education Department posts FY 2026 ‘Basic Needs for Postsecondary Students’ grant details ($10M)
items.summary: The U.S. Department of Education posted FY 2026 funding details for the Basic Needs for Postsecondary Students Program, with $10,000,000 in appropriations and $10,000,000 estimated available funds. ED lists an estimated award range of $1 million to $5 million over a 48-month project period and expects 2 to 10 awards.

items.title: SSI June payments go out June 1; SSA Social Security schedule continues June 3/10/17/24
items.summary: The Social Security Administration’s payment schedule means SSI recipients are paid on June 1, 2026 (the first of the month, since it’s a weekday). Social Security retirement and SSDI payments for most beneficiaries follow the Wednesday schedule in June—June 10, June 17, and June 24—while people who started benefits before May 1997 (and some dual SSI/Social Security recipients) are paid June 3.

items.title: CBPP analysis: SNAP enrollment down by more than 3.5 million since July 2025 as new rules take effect
items.summary: A Center on Budget and Policy Priorities analysis cited in reporting found SNAP participation fell by more than 3.5 million people between July 2025 and February 2026 after eligibility changes in the One Big Beautiful Bill Act. The report also notes the law includes $187 billion in SNAP cuts and expands work requirements to more groups (including some adults ages 55–64).
items.source: CNBC (citing Center on Budget and Policy Priorities analysis)
